data.name: A few things to check before starting the upgrade.
data.value: A few things to check before starting the upgrade.

data.name: A few things to do once the upgrade completes.
data.value: A few things to do once the upgrade has completed.

data.name: A reboot is required to load the new kernel and complete the upgrade.
data.value: A reboot is required to load the new kernel and complete the upgrade.

data.name: Although the upgrade is safe, always back up important files before major system changes.
data.value: Although the upgrade is safe, always back up important files before major system changes.

data.name: At least 3 GB free space
data.value: At least 3 GB of free space

data.name: Brand-new AnduinOS Appearance app for theming and AnduinOS Firewall app for easy network security management.
data.value: Brand-new AnduinOS Appearance app for theming and AnduinOS Firewall app for easy network security management.

data.name: Check that your system now reports AnduinOS 2.0.0.
data.value: Check that your system now reports AnduinOS 2.0.0.

data.name: Check that your system now reports AnduinOS 2.0.1.
data.value: Check that your system now reports AnduinOS 2.0.1.

data.name: Five years of security updates and package maintenance — supported until April 2031.
data.value: Five years of security updates and package maintenance — supported until April 2031.

data.name: Follow this guide to upgrade from AnduinOS 1.3.x or 1.4.x to 2.0.0 Resolute Raccoon.
data.value: Follow this guide to upgrade from AnduinOS 1.3.x or 1.4.x to 2.0.0 Resolute Raccoon.

data.name: Follow this guide to upgrade from AnduinOS 1.3.x or 1.4.x to 2.0.1 Resolute Raccoon.
data.value: Follow this guide to upgrade from AnduinOS 1.3.x or 1.4.x to 2.0.1 Resolute Raccoon.

data.name: Fully compatible with apt — Apkg acts as a transparent apt server, seamlessly integrating with existing apt workflows and updates.
data.value: Fully compatible with apt — Apkg acts as a transparent apt server, seamlessly integrating with existing apt workflows and updates.

data.name: If the command is not found, run the system upgrade tool first to update to 1.3.9 or 1.4.2.
data.value: If the command is not found, run the system upgrade tool first to update to 1.3.9 or 1.4.2.

data.name: If you encounter any issues during the upgrade, we're here to help.
data.value: If you encounter any issues during the upgrade, we're here to help.

data.name: Installs the GNOME 50 desktop, Apkg package manager, AnduinOS themes, fonts, wallpapers, and core applications.
data.value: Installs the GNOME 50 desktop, Apkg package manager, AnduinOS themes, fonts, wallpapers, and core applications.

data.name: Latest hardware support with improved scheduler, better power efficiency, and filesystem performance.
data.value: Latest hardware support with improved scheduler, better power efficiency, and file system performance.

data.name: Make sure you are on the latest 1.3.x or 1.4.x version. Run the upgrade tool to update first if needed.
data.value: Make sure you are on the latest 1.3.x or 1.4.x version. Run the upgrade tool to update first if needed.

data.name: Modern declarative package management replacing traditional apt workflows.
data.value: Modern declarative package management replacing traditional apt workflows.

data.name: Moves from Ubuntu 25.10 (plucky) to Ubuntu 26.04 (resolute) with optimized mirror selection.
data.value: Switches from Ubuntu 25.10 (plucky) to Ubuntu 26.04 (resolute) with optimized mirror selection.

data.name: New wallpapers, refined themes, optimized performance tuning, and up-to-date software packages across the board.
data.value: New wallpapers, refined themes, optimised performance tuning, and up-to-date software packages across the board.

data.name: Packages will be downloaded during upgrade. A stable connection is required.
data.value: Packages will be downloaded during the upgrade. A stable connection is required.

data.name: Performs a full distribution upgrade with retry logic, DKMS cleanup, and safe fallback handling.
data.value: Performs a full distribution upgrade with retry logic, DKMS cleanup, and safe fallback handling.

data.name: Resolute Raccoon brings major improvements across the entire system.
data.value: Resolute Raccoon delivers major improvements across the entire system.

data.name: Restores your PPAs (upgrading those compatible with resolute), removes orphans, and cleans the package cache.
data.value: Restores your PPAs (upgrading those compatible with Resolute), removes orphaned packages, and clears the package cache.

data.name: Run a full upgrade to ensure all packages are at the latest resolute versions.
data.value: Run a full upgrade to ensure all packages are at the latest Resolute versions.

data.name: Run as root or with sudo
data.value: Run as root or with sudo

data.name: Run this single command in your terminal. The upgrade is fully automated — no manual intervention needed.
data.value: Run this single command in your terminal. The upgrade is fully automated — no manual intervention is required.

data.name: Safely backs up your current APT sources and temporarily removes PPAs to ensure a clean upgrade.
data.value: Safely backs up your current APT sources and temporarily removes PPAs to ensure a clean upgrade.

data.name: The desktop will restart during upgrade. Run inside tmux so the terminal survives GUI restarts.
data.value: The desktop will restart during the upgrade. Run inside tmux so the terminal survives GUI restarts.

data.name: The latest GNOME desktop with improved performance, smoother animations, and new features.
data.value: The latest GNOME desktop with improved performance, smoother animations, and new features.

data.name: The upgrade may take 30-60 minutes. Plug in to avoid interruptions.
data.value: The upgrade may take 30–60 minutes. Plug in to avoid interruptions.

data.name: The upgrade needs space for package downloads and backups. Ensure you have enough free space in /.
data.value: The upgrade requires space for package downloads and backups. Ensure you have sufficient free space in /.

data.name: The upgrade script handles everything automatically. Here is what it does.
data.value: The upgrade script handles everything automatically. Here is what it does.

data.name: Verifies disk space, configures unattended mode, and ensures the system is fully updated.
data.value: Checks disk space, configures unattended mode, and ensures the system is fully updated.
